name: Chymopapain
description: Chymopapain is a proteolytic enzyme isolated from the latex of papaya (Carica papaya), it is a medication used to treat herniated lower lumbar discs in the spine. The sale and distribution of chymopapain was discontinued in the United States on January 27, 2003. Serious side effects from the use of chymopapain include anaphylaxis, paralysis of the legs, or death. [Wikipedia]
indication: Chymopapain injections should preferably be given under local, rather than general, anaesthesia. The dose for a single intervertebral disc is 2 to 4 nanokatals, with a maximum dose per patient of 8 nanokatals. [Wikipedia]
toxicity: Intradiscal injection of chymopapain is associated with anaphylactic reactions. While the main culprit has not yet been identified, it is hypothesized that purified preparations of chymopapain would have a reduced incidence of anaphylactic reactions.
mechanismOfAction: Chymopapain is a basic protein and thus attaches itself to acidic glycosaminoglycans by electrostatic binding. This interaction is supposed to displace water and cause the collapse of proteoglycans and electroprecipitation.
